prompt:
A compact M.2 PoE+ HAT PCB resting on an anti-static mat: one end features an RJ-45 Ethernet jack with magnetics for power-over-Ethernet, and the center of the board has an M.2 slot with a small NVMe SSD partially inserted. A 40-pin header footprint or stacking connector is visible along one edge, indicating that the board can sit on top of a single-board computer. The PoE+ and M.2 components are clearly identifiable, but any silkscreen text is replaced by simple icons.

Near-future solarpunk hardware lab aesthetic with warm lighting, a few tools, and a small single-board computer blurred in the background. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering with no brand logos or legible writing. The composition should clearly communicate that this board provides both power over Ethernet and an M.2 slot for NVMe drives.
